archetype: Bovine Genomic Prediction and Selection Orchestration System (BGP-SOS)
domain: Dairy Cattle Genomics and Quantitative Genetics
description: A system that ingests raw SNP chip genotype data, pedigree records, and phenotypic performance metrics (milk yield, fat/protein percentages, health events) from thousands of dairy herds to compute genomic estimated breeding values (GEBVs) using single-step GBLUP. It is used by geneticists at breeding companies and progressive farm managers to make real-time mating decisions and rank replacement heifers, with the core difficulty being the integration of sparse, noisy field data with dense genomic markers under strict USDA and industry-defined genetic evaluation protocols.
rules: MUST process and validate raw genotype files in proprietary Axiom BovineSNP50/HD array formats and convert them to PLINK .bed/.bim/.fam for analysis.
MUST implement and adhere to the USDA's genomic evaluation schedule and trait definition standards (e.g., Net Merit $, Cheese Merit $) as published by the Council on Dairy Cattle Breeding (CDCB).
MUST maintain an immutable audit trail for all GEBV calculations, linking every result to a specific version of the genetic evaluation model, reference population, and SNP chip calibration file.

MUST enforce breed-specific constraints and mandatory exclusion rules (e.g., carrier status for recessive defects like CVM, BLAD, MULE) per Holstein Association USA and other breed association bylaws.

MUST provide deterministic, reproducible evaluation runs for regulatory and royalty audit purposes, preventing any 'black box' ML model drift from the officially published quantitative genetic models.
anti_patterns: DO NOT treat this as a generic ML pipeline—using a TensorFlow/PyTorch model without a single-step GBLUP foundation will produce predictions that are biologically invalid and non-compliant with industry standards.
DO NOT ignore the multi-generational pedigree structure; failing to correctly merge sparse historic paper pedigree records with dense genomic data destroys the accuracy of the relationship matrix.
DO NOT process SNP chip data without a rigorous quality control/calibration step against the breed-specific base population allele frequencies, leading to systematic bias in GEBVs.
DO NOT design for batch-only processing; mating decisions require low-latency (<5 sec) GEBV lookups for individual animals during on-farm consultation.
key_technical_challenges: Computing the inverse of the huge, sparse combined pedigree-genomic relationship matrix (H^-1) for tens of thousands of animals in a single-step GBLUP framework, requiring optimized sparse linear algebra and memory management.

Integrating real-time, asynchronous phenotypic data streams (e.g., daily milk meter yields, automated health alerts from farm management software) with quarterly genomic evaluation cycles without re-running the entire model.

Designing a data pipeline that handles the 'long tail' of rare breeds and mixed-breed animals with incomplete or non-standard SNP chip data while still producing statistically robust, usable predictions.

recommended_stack.language: Python (with NumPy/SciPy/Scikit-allel) – dominant bioinformatics stack with libraries for genomic data manipulation and linear algebra.
recommended_stack.framework: FastAPI – for high-performance, async API endpoints to serve individual animal GEBV lookups and trigger batch jobs.
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL + PostGIS – for storing complex, hierarchical pedigree relationships and geolocated farm metadata with ACID compliance for audit trails.
recommended_stack.cache: Redis – for caching frequently requested GEBV results and intermediate genomic relationship matrix blocks for the active reference population.
recommended_stack.message_broker: RabbitMQ – to decouple the ingestion of raw farm data files (genotypes, phenotypes) from the computationally intensive, scheduled genomic evaluation runs.
special_services: A dedicated 'Genetic Evaluation Engine' service wrapping compiled Fortran/C++ code (like BLUPF90 family programs) for core GBLUP computations.
A 'SNP Chip Calibration & QC' microservice that normalizes raw intensity data against breed-specific base pairs and filters markers by call rate, MAF, and Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium.

compliance_flags: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) – Dairy Program genomic evaluation participation rules.
Council on Dairy Cattle Breeding (CDCB) – Data submission and trait definition standards.
Breed Association Bylaws (e.g., Holstein USA) – Carrier status reporting and genetic defect exclusion regulations.
Animal Welfare Act / State-specific Dairy Herd Improvement (DHI) program data privacy agreements.
